learning_outcomes.description: Understand the basic theoretical underpinning of several major theories of psychotherapy, including their historical development and modern practice.
Understand and practice the fundamental elements involved in developing a psychotherapy treatment formulation.
Understand ethical issues in the practice of psychotherapy.
Understand how research on psychotherapy outcomes and efficacies is conducted
Reflect on the personal impacts of various psychotherapy strategies
Understand basic principles of counselling skills and processes.
Build your repertoire of self-care behaviours and skills

Understand the importance of cultural safety and culturally respectful communication and how these concepts may be applied when working with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, families and communities.

assessments.due_date:
In-semester Saturday

5/09/2026 - 19/09/2026

You will be advised at a later date when the exam will take place during the in-semester exam period.
assessments.task_description:
The in-person examination will be 60 minutes and consist of 40 multiple choice questions. The questions will cover all lecture and tutorial content from Week 1 to 5 inclusive. The exam is closed book.

Guidelines on use of AI:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The video case study assignment information and marking criteria will be provided on Blackboard and discussed in depth during tutorials. 

Students will submit a video of themselves doing a brief first therapy session role-play. Students will then answer questions relating to their use of counselling skills in the role-play, identification of case formulation elements in the video, and discussion of treatment planning.

 Guidelines on use of AI:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Via Turnitin as well as video submission on Blackboard. NOTE: You will no longer receive an automatic submission receipt from Turnitin confirming your assignment has been received. You should download a copy of the digital receipt from the Assignment Dashboard and keep this as a record.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The Portfolio must be completed and submitted in accord with the portfolio information sheet. You will be given a Word document template to complete which contains weekly self-practice, self-reflection and self-care exercises. There are 10 exercises to complete.

Guidelines on use of AI:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
End of Semester Exam Period

7/11/2026 - 21/11/2026

You will be advised at a later date when the exam will take place during the exam period.
assessments.task_description:
The in-person examination will be 2 hours and consist of 80 multiple choice questions. The questions will cover all lecture and tutorial content from Week 6 to 12 inclusive. The exam is closed book.

Guidelines on use of AI:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
